samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit multiplier with pipelining technique.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description></parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Register declarations
        reg [15:0] mul_out_reg;
        reg [7:0] mul_a_reg;
        reg [7:0] mul_b_reg;
        reg [15:0] temp[7:0];
        reg [15:0] sum[3:0];
        
        // Assignments for output enable signal
        assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[7];
        // Sequential logic block triggered on positive edge of clock or negative edge of reset
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set all registers to zero
                mul_en_out_reg <= 0;
                mul_a_reg <= 0;
                mul_b_reg <= 0;
                mul_out_reg <= 0;
                temp[0] <= 0;
                temp[1] <= 0;
                temp[2] <= 0;
                temp[3] <= 0;
                temp[4] <= 0;
                temp[5] <= 0;
                temp[6] <= 0;
                temp[7] <= 0;
                sum[0] <= 0;
                sum[1] <= 0;
                sum[2] <= 0;
                sum[3] <= 0;
            end else begin
                // Positive edge of clock: Update registers based on conditions
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[6:0], mul_en_in};
                mul_a_reg <= mul_a_reg & {{7{mul_en_in}}, mul_a}; // Conditional assignment for multiplicand
                mul_b_reg <= mul_b_reg & {{7{mul_en_in}}, mul_b}; // Conditional assignment for multiplier
                temp[0] <= mul_a_reg & {{8{mul_b_reg[0]}}, 8'h00}; // Generate partial product for bit 0
                temp[1] <= mul_a_reg & {{7{mul_b_reg[1]}}, 8'h00} << 1; // Generate partial product for bit 1
                temp[2] <= mul_a_reg & {{6{mul_b_reg[2]}}, 8'h00} << 2; // Generate partial product for bit 2
                temp[3] <= mul_a_reg & {{5{mul_b_reg[3]}}, 8'h00} << 3; // Generate partial product for bit 3
                temp[4] <= mul_a_reg & {{4{mul_b_reg[4]}}, 8'h00} << 4; // Generate partial product for bit 4
                temp[5] <= mul_a_reg & {{3{mul_b_reg[5]}}, 8'h00} << 5; // Generate partial product for bit 5
                temp[6] <= mul_a_reg & {{2{mul_b_reg[6]}}, 8'h00} << 6; // Generate partial product for bit 6
                temp[7] <= mul_a_reg & {{1{mul_b_reg[7]}}, 8'h00} << 7; // Generate partial product for bit 7
                
                // Perform addition operations for partial sums
                sum[0] <= temp[0] + temp[1]; // Add partial products 0 and 1
                sum[1] <= temp[2] + temp[3]; // Add partial products 2 and 3
                sum[2] <= temp[4] + temp[5]; // Add partial products 4 and 5
                sum[3] <= temp[6] + temp[7]; // Add partial products 6 and 7
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3]; // Accumulate partial sums to get final product
            end
        end
        
        // Assign output product based on output enable signal
        assign mul_out = mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 0;
    </logic_description>
    <implementation></implementation>
    <task></task>
</module>
